text: We herein describe the gold(I) catalyzed sulfination of arylboronic acids using a hitherto unknown mechanistic pathway where the gold(I)–heteroatom bond is utilized to generate in situ the sulfinate ion. This catalyst system can be extended towards the synthesis of aryl sulfones and sulfonamides, two common pharmacophores utilized extensively in drug design and synthesis.
